case: 16x16 side-view sword (SwordsBench-style static, scoped single example)
step_toggled: the live_save_preview see-step in the draw -> see -> fix loop
method: Drew the sword once WITHOUT the see-step (blind, executed from a fixed coordinate plan, 33 px) = variant B. Then applied the perception loop on the same sprite: live_save_preview (16x upscale + coordinate gutter + inline image + components Set-of-Mark), inspected the rendered pixels, and fixed the defect the render revealed (39 px outline) = variant A. B's pixels are a strict subset of A's, so the only difference is what the see-step added.

without.outline: absent (palette index 0, the cool-dark outline, was never placed)

with.outline: 1px hue-shifted cool-dark #2a2e3a around the full silhouette (rules/02)

defect_caught_by_seeing: The blind draw left the silhouette with NO outline (the outline colour was never used); the rendered preview made it obvious. rules/02 + rules/06 want a 1px hue-shifted dark outline so the sprite reads at 100%.

objective_delta.spatial_defects_components_orphans_offpa: 0 -> 0 (the blind draw was already clean: 1 connected component, no orphans, fully on-palette)

verdict: On this scoped case, perception's MEASURABLE contribution was a readability fix (the missing outline), not error-correction — because a carefully coordinate-planned 16x16 sprite leaves no objective spatial defects to catch. The see-step did surface and let me fix a real rules-defined defect the blind pass missed.
caveats: N=1, single scoped case (matches the Path-2 row's bar).
A single agent drew AND assessed (no independent blind LLM judge in this run), so the readability improvement is rules-grounded but not externally scored.

The objective spatial-defect metric (orphans / off-palette) was uninformative here because careful coordinate planning is itself a form of verification; perception's headline error-CATCHING value (orphans, asymmetry, off-by-one) is better shown on a complex/freehand sprite or with an independent judge -- still the open Path-1 measurement.
evidence: path1_without.png (variant B preview, with component overlay)
path1_with.png (variant A preview, with component overlay)
